steps.title: Set up the inequality test
steps.content:
Every row in the correct table must satisfy $y\lt5x+6$. Since all choices use $x=3,5,7$, compute the upper bounds once:
$$5(3)+6=21,\quad 5(5)+6=31,\quad 5(7)+6=41$$

steps.title: Check the working table against the bounds
steps.content:
The correct answer has $y$-values $17,27,37$. Compare them row by row:
$$17\lt21,\quad 27\lt31,\quad 37\lt41$$
All three rows work.

steps.title: Eliminate the other tables
steps.content: The other tables each have a row that is not strictly below its bound: one has $35 ot\lt31$, another has $25 ot\lt21$, and another has $21 ot\lt21$. The table with $y$-values $17,27,37$ satisfies every row, so A is correct.
